title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 57.
content:
There was an Old Man of Cape Horn,
    Who wished he had never been born;
    So he sat on a chair,
    Till he died of despair,
    That dolorous Man of Cape Horn.
meaning:
Edward Lear's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Book Of Nonsense Limerick 57."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
